task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot showing the Tor Browser open with 'check.torproject.org' in the address bar and the page loaded (typically showing 'Congratulations. This browser is configured to use Tor.' and an IP address).
Screenshot showing the Tor Browser open with 'browserleaks.com/webrtc' in the address bar and the WebRTC leak test page loaded.
Screenshot showing the Tor Browser open with 'browserleaks.com/canvas' in the address bar and the Canvas fingerprinting test page loaded.
Screenshot showing the Tor Browser open with 'coveryourtracks.eff.org' in the address bar and the EFF tracking test page loaded.

task_completion.description: Create the compatibility report with the correct title and date
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of a text editor or terminal showing the file '/home/ga/Documents/tor-compatibility-report.txt' being edited. The content must include the exact title '=== TOR BROWSER COMPATIBILITY REPORT ===' and the current date.

task_completion.description: Include sections 1 and 2 with substantive observations
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the report showing '1. TOR CONNECTION STATUS' (must include an actual exit IP address) and '2. WEBRTC LEAK TEST' with text describing the test results.

task_completion.description: Include sections 3, 4, and 5 with substantive observations
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the report showing '3. CANVAS FINGERPRINT TEST', '4. EFF COVER YOUR TRACKS', and '5. SUMMARY' with text describing the test results and a final conclusion.

integrity.description: The agent used the Tor Browser GUI to visit the sites
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the distinct Tor Browser interface (often with the onion logo or specific Tor toolbar elements) rather than a standard Firefox/Chrome browser or terminal curl commands.

integrity.description: The report contains real test results rather than placeholders
integrity.visual_evidence: The report text must contain specific details gathered from the sites (e.g., a real IP format like 'x.x.x.x', specific WebRTC/Canvas statuses) and not placeholder text like '[Insert IP here]' or verbatim copy-pasting of the task prompt.

integrity.description: The agent manually gathered information and wrote the report
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory should show the agent switching between the browser (to read results) and the text editor (to write the report), rather than executing a pre-written script that magically generates the entire report without viewing the pages.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target report path: /home/ga/Documents/tor-compatibility-report.txt. Required URLs to visit: check.torproject.org, browserleaks.com/webrtc, browserleaks.com/canvas, coveryourtracks.eff.org. The report must contain real observations from these live sites over the Tor network.
